mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.18.0.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to define its custom resources in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace, allowing you to manage it through Kubernetes.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
Creating an example CR allows you to deploy a vLLM instance with specific configurations.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This command creates an example vLLM instance using the GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM instance is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM instances in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all vLLM instances and configurations, leading to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process will upgrade the vLLM installation to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl drain example-vllm --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl uncordon example-vllm

```

> This command drains the vLLM pod to prepare for upgrade.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> This command uncordons the vLLM pod after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM release may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the installation with 'helm list --namespace vllm' and ensure the namespace is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create VLLM instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD for vLLM may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD with 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM instance may be misconfigured or the model may not be available.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM instance.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the vLLM CR or scale the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved PagedAttention performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM instance listed with 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm' and its status should be 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
